channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Federal workers start to get a new email demanding their accomplishments
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal employees are starting to receive another email requiring them to explain their recent accomplishments, a renewed attempt by President Donald Trump and billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk to demand answers from the government workforce.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Video shows implosion of iconic Six Flags rollercoaster Kingda Ka
item.description: The king has fallen. The legendary Kingda Ka ride at Six Flags Great Adventure in New Jersey was imploded on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump expected to sign order designating English as the official language of the US
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order designating English as the official language of the United States, according to the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Jury finds Illinois landlord guilty of murder, hate crime in 2023 attack on Palestinian American boy
item.description: JOLIET, Ill. (AP) — A jury found an Illinois landlord guilty of murder and hate crime charges Friday for the brutal killing of a 6-year-old whose mother rented rooms in the man’s home, an attack that spiked fears over anti-Muslim discrimination in the earliest days of the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and MELISSA PEREZ WINDER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Iowa's governor signs a bill removing gender identity protections from the state’s civil rights code
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa became the first U.S. state to remove gender identity protections from its civil rights code on Friday when Gov. Kim Reynolds signed into law a bill that opponents say will expose transgender people and other Iowans to discrimination in all aspects of daily life.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New York's governor orders college to remove Palestinian studies job listing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York's governor ordered a state school to remove a job posting for a Palestinian studies teaching position this week, saying she wanted to ensure “antisemitic theories” would not be taught.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump-Zelensky clash highlights Congress at a crossroads over foreign policy
item.description: The explosive clash between President Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in the White House on Friday has sparked a firestorm on Capitol Hill, pitting “America First” Republicans against Ukraine backers from both parties in the ferocious battle over the future of U.S. foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: DOD sends over 1,000 additional troops to help bolster southern border efforts
item.description: The Department of Defense is sending over 1,000 additional troops to the southern border to help bolster efforts to control illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: School bus caught on camera engulfed in flames after driver's ‘quick response’ saves students from inferno
item.description: An Ohio school bus driver was credited with saving 15 middle school students as a bus went up in flames. The inferno was caught on camera.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Haitian man charged in NC triple murder flew into US under Biden migrant flights program: ICE
item.description: ICE said a Haitian migrant, who had flown into the US as part of former President Biden's migrant flight program, was arrested and charged with triple murder in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Leonard Peltier remains defiant in AP interview, maintaining innocence and vowing continued activism
item.description: BELCOURT, N.D. (AP) — More than 50 years after a shootout on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation landed him in federal prison, Leonard Peltier remains defiant.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, are back in the US: What to know
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, arrived Thursday in the U.S. after authorities lifted travel restrictions on the siblings, who have millions of online followers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Zelenskyy's White House meeting with Trump, Vance turns to shouting match
item.description: A White House meeting that was intended to kick off negotiations over a deal that would offer the United States access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals unraveled Friday afternoon as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy clashed with President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance in the Oval Of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and JUSTIN SPIKE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Law school project finds slavery citations still being used today
item.description: EAST LANSING, Mich. (AP) — An 1842 U.S. Supreme Court ruling overturning the kidnapping conviction of a white man who seized a Black family and forced them into slavery south of the Mason-Dixon line is still being cited in American jurisprudence, 160 years after enslaved people throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Vance go off on Zelensky in contentious Oval Office spat
item.description: President Trump went off on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky during an extraordinary Oval Office meeting in front of cameras on Friday in which he raised his voice and called the foreign leader “disrespectful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KTLA 5

item.title: Best mullet? Best use of Taylor Swift? AP hands out its own movie awards ahead of the Oscars
item.description: Hollywood's never-ending awards season can make it easy to get a little lost in the run-of-the-mill “best” categories. Actor. Actress. Director. Picture. A great movie can be reduced to a single performance; those that are left out seem to simply vanish for a while.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE and LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's transportation department cancels new review step that could've slowed state projects
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration is backing off its demand for an extra layer of federal scrutiny whenever states seek even minor changes to their transportation plans, after the rescinded requirement sparked concern that some payments for roads, bridges and transit would be de
item.dc:creator: JEFF McMURRAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: I-40 to reopen at Tennessee-North Carolina state line
item.description: The four-mile stretch of I-40 on the Tennessee and North Carolina border will reopen to motor traffic Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Naomi Hillmer, NewsNation

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife tested negative for carbon monoxide, sheriff says
item.description: Actor Gene Hackman, his wife Betsy Arakawa, and their dog were found dead in their Santa Fe home, in a scene that one detective had deemed "suspicious enough in nature to require a thorough search and investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fallon Fischer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he plans to add White House ballroom, pave part of the Rose Garden
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said he is planning to add a ballroom to the White House and to pave part the Rose Garden, similar to a patio at his Mar-a-Lago property in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: WhatsApp down Friday, according to user reports
item.description: (KRON) -- Instant messaging service WhatsApp appears to have gone down Friday, according to Downdetector.com. User reports of problems with the chat app appeared to spike Friday morning with a majority of users -- 54% -- reporting problems with sending messages.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Washington man arrested for stealing $8M fishing boat with crew still on board, officials say
item.description: The suspect allegedly tried to steal a different boat earlier that morning, but its crew "ran him off the boat," police said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KRON4

item.title: Vice President JD Vance, a Catholic, acknowledges the pope's criticism of US immigration crackdown
item.description: Vice President JD Vance acknowledged Pope Francis’ criticism of the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigration, without responding to any of its specifics or to the pontiff’s apparent criticism of Vance’s own deployment of Catholic tradition to justify such policies.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida teen tries vandalizing ex's car, ends up painting neighbor's vehicle instead
item.description: The 18-year-old admitted she was upset with her ex-boyfriend and had decided to throw eggs at and spray-paint his vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: New Mexico woman fatally shot, accused of leaving teen in chicken coop
item.description: Patricia Madrid and her husband, 59-year-old Jammy Jackson, were arrested after police said they left a 15-year-old in a chicken coop for long periods of time.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Honeycutt, NewsNation

item.title: Former sheriff ‘flabbergasted’ by alleged gun seizure incident
item.description: “It sounds like robbery,” Wilson’s attorney, Bobby Bailey, said. “To go to a person’s personal job and to take his personal items; to me, that’s robbery.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Map: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found as death toll climbs
item.description: An Indiana-based dairy company has recalled potentially affected shakes that were distributed nationwide to long-term care facilities.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Meet the only 2 passengers from the 'private' Southwest flight out of Utah
item.description: "It was so cool. It was a really fun experience," Natalie Wolfley said. "Obviously, once in a lifetime, probably -- I don't know if I'll ever have that kind of experience again.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, NewsNation

item.title: Man arrested in Nashville for impersonating law enforcement
item.description: According to police, 31-year-old Leart Bytyqi was identified as the man who tried to pull over a driver by flashing a badge out the car window.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Guerry, NewsNation

item.title: EPA backtracks on Trump comment about cutting staff by 65% but says major spending cuts are coming
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Despite a comment by President Donald Trump, the Environmental Protection Agency does not plan to reduce its staff by 65%, the White House and the agency said Thursday, though major budget cuts are likely.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NOAA begins firing hundreds of staffers
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) began the process of firing hundreds of probationary workers Thursday, the latest mass job cuts within federal agencies, three sources with knowledge of internal operations told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A look at Louisiana's Mardi Gras festivities through the years
item.description: After a weekslong pre-Lenten bash — complete with elaborate parades that meander through New Orleans, shimmery beads tossed from floats, streets lined with costumed revelers, lavish balls and seafood boils — Louisiana's 2025 Carnival Season is approaching its grand conclusion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman accused of marrying her biological father
item.description: A Tell City woman is facing charges in Vanderburgh County for allegedly marrying her biological father.
item.dc:creator: Zach McKnight, WFLA, NBC
